dead-path.description: Attempted to animate shape fill opacity via p:anim on fill.opacity attrName with a fltVal tavLst. PPT parses the XML as valid OOXML but the playback engine drops the animation entirely — the shape renders at its authored opacity with zero transition.
dead-path.source: .visual_tmp/transparency_experiment.py; legacy VML attrName from binary PPT spec

replacement.note: Use preset 9 Transparency — p:set style.opacity + p:animEffect filter="image" prLst="opacity: X" in one cTn. This is the native shape fill opacity path PPT actually honors.

dead-path.description: Attempted to animate shape stroke opacity via p:anim on stroke.opacity. Legacy VML attrName; no modern PPT animation primitive exists for partial-range stroke opacity.

replacement.note: No native path. For SVG animate[stroke-opacity] fall back to EMF rasterization. Binary 0-or-1 stroke opacity can be approximated by toggling stroke.on via p:set.

dead-path.description: Attempted to animate shape stroke (line) width via p:anim on stroke.weight. ECMA-376 documents stroke.weight as an animatable attribute but PowerPoint's modern playback engine silently drops it. The LineFormat.Weight VBA property exists for static formatting only.
dead-path.source: .visual_tmp/transparency_experiment.py stroke_weight candidate; research notes on VML legacy

replacement.note: No native path. For SVG animate[stroke-width] fall back to EMF rasterization or discrete set steps via static LineFormat.Weight. Fundamentally impossible as an interpolated animation in current PPT.

dead-path.description: Alternate spelling of stroke.weight. Also silently dropped — PPT's attrName resolver accepts either but neither fires at playback.

replacement.note: Same as stroke.weight — no native path, EMF fallback only.

shape.context: without sibling animClr/animClr/set children in the same cTn

dead-path.description: Attempted to animate text font size via a lone p:anim to="1.5" on style.fontSize inside a standalone clickEffect wrapper. Does NOT fire in isolation. PPT only honors style.fontSize when it appears alongside the full preset 28 compound (animClr on style.color + animClr on fillcolor + set on fill.type + anim on style.fontSize) as sibling children of ONE cTn. Remove any of the siblings and the font size animation silently stops working.
dead-path.source: .visual_tmp/transparency_experiment.py font_size retest — 3 iterations across simple, full-compound, and final working (to="4" for visibility) configurations

replacement.slot: emph/compound with fill_color + custom anim-style.fontSize child
replacement.note: When SVG requires text font-size animation, emit the full preset 28 compound: use emph/compound + fill_color fragment + a raw style.fontSize anim child. Size-only animations (no color change) are NOT supported natively.

shape.context: outside preset 9 Transparency wrapper (no paired p:set style.opacity)

dead-path.description: The animEffect filter="image" element with prLst="opacity: X" is NOT a standalone entrance/exit effect. It only fires when paired with a p:set on style.opacity (to the same target value) inside a preset 9 Transparency cTn. Using the filter in any other preset wrapper or without the paired set results in the shape rendering at its current opacity with no transition.
dead-path.source: .visual_tmp/transparency_experiment.py transparency_pulse with autoRev — emitted the animEffect but not the paired set; animation did not fire

replacement.note: Always pair p:animEffect filter="image" prLst="opacity: X" with a p:set on style.opacity → "X" in the same cTn. The emph/transparency oracle slot bakes this pairing in.

dead-path.description: Attempted to animate shape opacity by emitting p:anim on style.opacity with a p:tavLst containing fltVal keyframes (e.g. from 0.2 to 1.0). style.opacity accepts p:set for instantaneous values (used as the preset 9 Transparency primer) but rejects p:anim-with-tavLst at playback. Legacy attrName from binary PPT spec.

replacement.slot: emph/transparency (partial range) or entr/fade / exit/fade (0↔1 range)
replacement.note: For partial-range opacity holds use emph/transparency. For full 0→1 or 1→0 fade use entr/filter_effect FILTER=fade or exit/filter_effect FILTER=fade.
